name: Send daily AI market briefings to email and Discord using AlphaAI and GPT

parameters.content:
## Send daily AI market briefings to email and Discord using AlphaAI and GPT

### How it works

1. A Schedule Trigger fires every weekday at 7am, and a Set node defines the watchlist, the relevance floor and the red flag threshold.
2. Four parallel HTTP Request nodes pull per ticker news, a 7 day sentiment tally, a 30 day SEC Form 4 insider summary and trending stories from the AlphaAI news API.
3. A Code node merges the branches into one payload and derives red flags deterministically, so the AI narrates but cannot invent alerts.
4. An LLM chain writes the morning brief from that payload with any chat model.
5. The workflow emails the full brief as HTML and, when red flags exist, pings a Discord channel immediately.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Create a free AlphaAI API key at alphai.io (Account, API keys) and add it as one shared Bearer Auth credential on the four AlphaAI nodes, without the "Bearer " prefix.
- [ ] Add your OpenAI credential on the "OpenAI Chat Model" node, or swap it for any other chat model.
- [ ] Set your SMTP credential and the from and to addresses on the "Send Briefing Email" node.
- [ ] Optional: paste a Discord channel webhook URL into the "Send Urgent Discord Ping" node, or delete that branch.
- [ ] Put your tickers into the "Set Watchlist and Settings" node and activate the workflow.

### Customization

Up to 6 tickers fits the AlphaAI Free tier (at most 19 calls per run). Adjust the thresholds in the Set node or edit the prompt in the LLM chain.

## Set schedule and watchlist

Runs every weekday at 7am and defines the tickers, the relevance floor and the red flag threshold.

## Fetch AlphaAI market data

Splits the watchlist and calls four AlphaAI endpoints in parallel: per ticker news, 7 day sentiment, 30 day insider activity and trending stories.

## Merge and assemble the payload

Waits for all branches, zips them into one payload and derives red flags deterministically, so the AI cannot invent alerts.

## Write the brief with AI

An LLM chain turns the payload into a morning brief. Swap the model node for any chat model.

## Render email and alerts

Builds the HTML email and formats red flag embed cards for Discord.

## Deliver by email and Discord

Emails the full brief, and posts red flags to a Discord webhook when any exist.

parameters.text:
=You are a buy-side analyst writing a pre-open morning briefing for a private investor. Below is a JSON payload with (a) the investor's watchlist — per ticker: high-relevance news from roughly the last day (each with an AI relevance score 1-10 and per-ticker sentiment), a 7-day sentiment tally (bullish/neutral/bearish article counts) and a 30-day SEC Form 4 insider summary; (b) market-wide trending stories; (c) deterministic red flags (bearish score>=8 news or notable insider filings, last 24h).

Write the briefing in Markdown, under 600 words, in this exact structure:

# Morning Market Briefing — <date>
## TL;DR
Up to 3 bullets — only what changes decisions today.
## Red flags
Only if red_flags is non-empty: one bullet per flag — what happened and what to verify before the open. Omit the whole section when there are none.
## Your watchlist
One short block per ticker in `watchlist`: lead with the read (bullish/bearish/mixed and why), cite specifics — relevance scores, insider dollar values, sentiment tallies. If `quiet_tickers` is non-empty, end the section with exactly one line: "Quiet: <quiet_tickers joined with commas> — no significant coverage." If `quiet_tickers` is empty, write no Quiet line at all.
## Market pulse
2-4 trending stories that matter beyond the watchlist; one line each on why they matter, with the article link.

Rules: be specific and quantitative, no filler, no generic advice, no disclaimers. Never invent facts, numbers or tickers — mention ONLY tickers present in the payload. Whenever you reference an article, link it as a Markdown link whose anchor text is the article's title, like [Apple Intelligence Fails To Ignite Upgrade Activity](https://alphai.io/news/article/…) — a shortened title is fine, but NEVER use "here", "Read more" or similar as anchor text, and use each url exactly as given in the payload.

Payload:
{{ $json.llm_input }}
